discussion_comments.comment_body:
Why using `Mini` prefix since everywhere in the code it is simply ContextMap?
Why using `Language` part as well?

Keep it uniform
We are trying to standardize diagram definitions, so please use lower case, and also add `-beta` suffix to it, that would imply that the syntax in the nearest future could have breaking changes

Is there any convention for files that includes only types ?
Something like `gantt.d.ts` or `gant.types.ts` ?
There probably is. I was just going off of what exists in other diagrams. But I do like the gantt.types.ts. I have a few other type files I would rename using that convention.

I did some research and I think the gantt.d.ts is a lot more conventional from what I can tell. [as shown here](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped)

I can go through and change each of the files to name.d.ts that are type declaration files.
You can proceed with `gant.d.ts` it’s better to migrate the rest of the types progressively

I've read that we should not be using `d.ts` to write types. 
A normal `ts` file should be enough, maybe `.type.ts` to convey it's types. 

https://x.com/mattpocockuk/status/1673017324587171842
Good to know! Is there anything explicating it in more than 280 characters?

I had read it in some other blog as well, a few months back, but couldn't get the source.

This guy has been making really good TS content for a long time, so I personally stopped using d.ts internally after I saw his post.
Will do I will change it to gantt.type.ts

```suggestion
export type DiagramStylesProvider = (options?: any) => string;
```

We use `UpperCamelCase` for type names.
Done.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
All new diagrams should have `-beta` suffix. 

```suggestion
      code: `wardley-beta
```

discussion_comments.comment_body: Fixed by the contributor

All the new diagrams should have `-beta` suffix, so changes to syntax can be added later. 

```suggestion
  return /^\s*treeView-beta/.test(txt);
```

Changing this would break other tools that might be using edgeIDs for other diagrams. Why do we need double underscore?
Maybe we can introduce a parameter for separator?

discussion_comments.comment_body:
Please use variable names matching our convention. 
There will be lots of changed lines, but that's okay. 

```suggestion
  config: MermaidConfig['gitGraph']
```
As this is not the default config, using this name is misleading. You can use `config` or `gitGraphConfig`.
Short names are spl request from @knsv

We're using `width, height` everywhere else. And because we're a library with downstream customers, I, personally would prefer explicit names.  It'll also make reading the code easier for new contributors. 

But upto @knsv
